title: midnight-craving crumbly uruguayan cornmeal empanadas
description: crumbly uruguayan empanadas with peppery cornmeal, perfect for midnight cravings. served with a side of spicy nuka-pickle sauce.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use flaky sea salt for added texture.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: use lukewarm water for easier dough handling.

ingredients.name: grated cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: optional, for an extra burst of flavor.

ingredients.name: corn kernels
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, for added crunch.

instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, salt, and black pepper. mix well to combine.
add vegetable shortening to the dry ingredients and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
gradually add lukewarm water to the mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is smooth and pliable.
divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of nuka-pickle sauce in the center of each disk.
fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
brush the tops of the empanadas with butter and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
use a bamboo steamer to cook the empanadas over boiling water for 15-20 minutes, until golden brown and crispy.
serve the empanadas hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and grated cheddar cheese if desired.
